title: Match ~100 functions
author: dwilliams27
body:
Hello!

I've been working the past ~2 weeks on developing a system for leveraging AI to automate finding matches for this repo. I ran it overnight a few times, and I think this first batch here should be ready for review. Repo I created for this [here](https://github.com/dwilliams27/gc-decomp)

I saw a few other commits in the repo co-authered by Claude, so assuming this is fair game.

I do not have much experience working on decompilation projects like this, so I apologize if some of what is in my PR here is "low quality". I've filtered out all of the "matches" that included any regression, and this seems to legitimately raise the global match rate by ~0.2%, so to me seems good.
I've also filtered out most of the cheats I've seen the LLMs use (inlining assembly lol), but totally possible there is more garbage here I did not catch.

More than happy to respond to feedback here; I'm sure there is a lot that could be done to improve the harness I've set up. Hoping to scale this up if maintainers agree that these results seem useful.

title: Match ~100 functions
author: PsiLupan
body: You can look at the Issues run and see most of the things that would need to be addressed, like missing function prototypes, C99 rule violations (variables must be declared at the top level of scope), etc. to give you an idea of what would need to be fixed.

title: Match ~100 functions
author: ribbanya
body: AI usage isn't a problem, as long as it matches and doesn't do anything too weird. You should join the [discord](https://discord.gg/hKx3FJJgrV), there's `#smash-bros-melee` and `#ai` as well.

title: Match ~100 functions
author: PsiLupan
body: This is just wrong. It's sbss, not sdata.

title: Match ~100 functions
author: PsiLupan
body: Same as the other. This is sbss, not sdata.

title: Match ~100 functions
author: ribbanya
body:
This kind of pointer arithmetic is slop. If it matches, then fine, but it's going to need to be cleaned up eventually by someone. If you can have Claude output `M2C_FIELD` that would be preferred. Ideally the proper fields of the struct (in this case `Fighter`) would be used, or created if they don't exist.

In this case, these are just callback fields (`death2_cb` etc.) so I'm not sure why it didn't opt to just use those. Are you running the functions through m2c before passing them to an LLM?

title: Match ~100 functions
author: ribbanya

title: Match ~100 functions
author: dwilliams27
body:
Gotcha. Yeah the agents have m2c available, but realizing I did not force it to start there. Will ensure that is forced as first step in flow, and add in logic on the verifier to reject common slop patterns (raw offset pointer arithmetic, NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs, and C99 loop-variable declarations) before accepting writes.

Appreciate this feedback! Will hop into the discord and try and also integrate other ideas I see in there to avoid generating more low quality work. Thanks for the review

title: Match ~100 functions
author: ribbanya
body: Besides the frequent field access problem, the regressions need to be resolved.

title: Match ~100 functions
author: ribbanya
body:
I'll accept these functions as they are for now, but I would really recommend not continuing with this workflow until m2c is integrated into it as a first pass, which others have done when using Claude (see https://github.com/doldecomp/melee/pull/2217#discussion_r2889680798).

---

I took a brief look at your project and noticed m2c is already integrated, so I'm not sure why this output so frequently opts for pointer arithmetic. Maybe someone more familiar with using Claude has an idea.
